title: March Mania 2026 — NCAA Basketball Prediction
summary: March Mania 2026 is Kaggle's annual NCAA men's basketball tournament prediction competition (prize: $50K). Jason's best submission is **v6 weighted ensemble at 0.02210 Stage 1 LB score**. Stage 2 (actual 2026 bracket) was also submitted using v6 pipeline. Later attempts (v11, v12) did not improve on v6.
what_worked: Weighted ensemble of diverse model depths (5–9) and types (XGBoost, LightGBM, LogReg, Elo baseline)
Four Factors features (eFG%, TOV%, ORB%, FT rate)
Elo ratings as a baseline signal
Tournament Experience as a feature (prior tournament appearances)
Pythagorean Win % for true strength estimation
Calibrated probabilities (Platt scaling on LogReg component)
what_didnt_work: v11: Adjusted efficiency metrics — may have introduced noise or leakage
v12: Recency weighting — tournament performance may not follow recent-season trends
open_questions: What was the Stage 2 final score?
Does conference strength need explicit encoding?
